name: Hunger
description: A food-related survival stat decays over play and applies thresholds, recovery, or penalties based on nutrition state.
design_notes: Hunger should create planning pressure without becoming constant menu maintenance. The important decisions are which survival_stat_id decays, how activity changes hunger_decay_per_minute, which food recovery sources are safe, and whether low hunger reduces stamina, healing, maximum health, or eventually causes starvation damage.
design_purpose: Encourage food gathering, cooking, farming, trading, or route planning.
Connect exploration length to preparation and inventory decisions.
Create long-term survival pressure that is readable before it becomes lethal.
player_fantasy: The player feels like food matters because preparation lets them stay active, heal, and travel farther.

parameters.name: survival_stat_id
parameters.description: Stat identifier used by decay, UI, recovery, save data, and status-effect rules.

parameters.name: hunger_decay_per_minute
parameters.description: Baseline hunger drain while the survival clock is advancing under normal activity.

parameters.name: activity_decay_multiplier
parameters.description: Multiplier applied during sprinting, combat, cold exposure, crafting, or resting states.

parameters.name: food_recovery_source_id
parameters.description: Consumable or meal source that restores hunger through an inventory transaction.

parameters.name: starvation_effect_id
parameters.description: Penalty applied when the hunger stat crosses its depletion threshold.

parameters.name: overfill_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule for consuming food when the hunger stat is already near its maximum.

edge_cases: A player consumes food while taking starvation damage in the same simulation tick.
Offline decay, sleeping, or fast travel advances hunger without giving the player a chance to respond.
Food restores hunger but also applies poison, spoilage, warmth, or hydration effects.
Multiplayer authority rejects food consumption after the client already played the eating animation.
common_bugs: Hunger decay runs during paused menus or loading screens because it reads a wall clock instead of survival clock state.
Food is removed from inventory but recovery is not applied when a capacity or status-effect transaction fails.
UI warning bands display stale hunger after server correction or save-load restoration.
Starvation damage stacks every frame instead of on a controlled tick interval.
balancing_notes: Fast decay can make survival tense, but it quickly becomes busywork if food sources are common and decisions are shallow.
Low hunger penalties are usually easier to tune than instant death because they give players recovery space.
Cooking and food preservation should reduce friction if hunger is a persistent long-session pressure.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear warning bands, optional numeric display, and non-color cues before hunger becomes dangerous.
Offer relaxed decay settings or assist modes for players who prefer exploration over resource upkeep.

implementation_notes.unity: Store hunger as a survival_stat_id record with current points, last_decay_tick, and active modifiers. Consumables should execute through one inventory transaction that removes item_instance_id, applies recovery, clamps overfill_policy_id, and logs the recovery_source_id.
implementation_notes.godot: Use an autoload survival service or Resource-backed stat model that advances from the gameplay clock, not UI process callbacks. Consumable scenes should request recovery through the service so save/load, warning bands, and status effects update together.
implementation_notes.unreal: A replicated survival component can own hunger, thresholds, and effects. The server should validate food use, apply recovery and poison or spoilage tags, then replicate the new stat and UI warning threshold to clients.
implementation_notes.web: Keep hunger decay in the simulation model with explicit pause and offline policies. Persist current points, last authority tick, and active starvation_effect_id so reloads do not duplicate damage or lose recent food recovery.

example_games.title: Don't Starve
example_games.note: Uses food pressure as a central survival planning loop.

example_games.title: Minecraft
example_games.note: Uses hunger to affect sprinting, healing, and food preparation.
